title: NOC 65210 – Support occupations in accommodation, travel and facilities set-up services | GTR Immigration
description: Support professionals under NOC 65210 are the behind-the-scenes experts who ensure Canada’s hospitality and event infrastructure functions seamlessly. From hotel bellhops and doormen to specialized conference and baggage handlers, these TEER 5 workers manage the logistics of guest comfort and event execution. In 2026, as Canada hosts record-breaking international conferences and sporting events, this role has become a vital entry point for the Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP) and specialized Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level5Major Group65 – Sales and service support occupationsSub-Major Group652 – Support occupations in food, accommodation and tourismMinor Group6521 – Accommodation and travel support occupationsNOC Code65210

duties: Carry luggage for guests to and from their rooms or to the loading area.
Assist guests with check-in and check-out procedures, such as summoning taxis or providing local information.
Set up rooms for meetings, conferences, and conventions according to specific floor plans.
Arrange furniture, audio-visual equipment, and refreshment stations for events.
Deliver room service orders, extra linens, or other requested items to guest rooms.
Maintain cleanliness and order in lobby areas and facility storage rooms.
Assist passengers during boarding and deboarding in travel terminals.
employment_requirements: No formal education is usually required, though some secondary school is often preferred.
On-the-job training is provided for all specific service and safety protocols.
High proficiency in English or French is essential for guest interaction.
Physical stamina and the ability to lift moderate to heavy items are mandatory.
Reliability and a professional, customer-focused attitude are highly valued by Canadian employers.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate a steady increase in job openings as the "Experience Economy" grows. As Canada builds more convention centers and luxury accommodations to meet global demand, the need for facilities set-up and guest support staff will remain a top priority for the hospitality industry.
labour_market_demand: The demand for accommodation and facilities support is Very Good, particularly in major tourism and business hubs. With the expansion of luxury hotel chains in Toronto, Vancouver, and Montreal, along with the high demand for seasonal staff in resort towns like Banff and Whistler, employers are actively seeking international talent to maintain high service standards.
High Accessibility: Minimal formal education requirements allow you to secure a job offer and begin your Canadian journey quickly.
Tip Potential: Roles such as bellhops and doormen often receive significant gratuities, supplementing base wages.
Networking: Interact with international travelers and business leaders in high-end corporate and hospitality environments.
Career Ladder: Gain foundational experience to move into Hotel Front Desk (NOC 64314) or Accommodation Service Management (NOC 60031).
pathways: Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion route for TEER 5 workers with job offers in the Maritimes.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): A dedicated pathway for those working in the tourism/hospitality sector for 9 consecutive months in BC.
Alberta Tourism and Hospitality Stream: A prioritized stream for workers currently employed in Alberta’s hospitality sector.
Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot (RNIP): Addressing labor needs in participating smaller communities.
